title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy skin
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy skin are infusions made from various plants and herbs that have anti-inflammatory, soothing, and healing properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort of itchy skin. Chamomile tea, for instance, contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce skin irritation and inflammation. Its antioxidant properties can also help speed up the skin's healing process. Another example is nettle tea, which is rich in minerals, vitamins, and antioxidants that can help soothe itchy skin and reduce redness and inflammation. By drinking these herbal teas or using them as a compress, individuals can find relief from itchy skin, improving their overall quality of life by reducing discomfort, promoting better sleep, and enhancing their confidence and well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with itchy skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and anti-pruritic properties that can soothe and alleviate skin irritation. The tea is made from the flowers, leaves, and stems of the yarrow plant, which are rich in azulene, a natural compound known for its calming effects on the skin. Drinking yarrow tea or applying it topically can help reduce inflammation, redness, and itchiness, providing relief to individuals suffering from various skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis. Additionally, yarrow tea has antimicrobial and astringent properties that can help cleanse and tighten the skin, promoting healing and preventing further irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce skin inflammation, which is crucial for alleviating itchy skin as itchiness often results from inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Astringent: Yarrow tea's astringent properties help tighten skin and reduce secretions, which is important for itchy skin as it can help dry out weeping or oozing rashes. The tannins in yarrow are responsible for its astringent properties.

Antipruritic: Yarrow tea has antipruritic properties, which means it can help alleviate itching. This is essential for itchy skin as it can provide immediate relief and reduce the urge to scratch. The lactones and essential oils in yarrow contribute to its antipruritic effects.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea's antimicrobial properties help prevent and treat skin infections, which is important for itchy skin as scratching can introduce bacteria and cause infections. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils, lactones, and flavonoids.

Analgesic: Yarrow tea's analgesic properties help relieve pain, which is crucial for itchy skin as constant scratching can cause pain and irritation. The sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids in yarrow contribute to its analgesic effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Crush the yarrow herb to release its oils, then place it in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the yarrow herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to discard the herb.
Drink the yarrow tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day, to relieve itchy skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Yarrow flowers are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them ideal for soothing itchy skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the yarrow plant have been used for centuries in teas to treat skin irritation and reduce itchiness.
Stems: Yarrow stems contain therapeutic oils and chemicals that can help alleviate itching and inflammation on the skin.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, which can help soothe itchy skin when consumed as a tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with itchy skin because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery and gel-like when mixed with water. This mucilage can help soothe and protect irritated skin, reducing inflammation and itchiness. The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow tea may also help to alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, which can cause itchy, dry, and flaky skin. Additionally, marshmallow tea has been shown to have a cooling effect on the skin, providing further relief from itchiness. Drinking marshmallow tea can also help to hydrate the skin from the inside out, promoting overall skin health and reducing the likelihood of itchiness.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage that forms a soothing film on the skin, reducing itchiness and inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in marshmallow tea help reduce skin inflammation, alleviating itchiness.
Antipruritic property: Marshmallow tea contains active constituents like tannins and rosmarinic acid that can help relieve itching directly.
Emollient property: The high mucilage content in marshmallow tea softens and soothes the skin, helping to alleviate itchiness caused by dryness.
Immune-boosting property: Marshmallow tea has been shown to stimulate the immune system, which can help the body fight off skin infections and irritations that may cause itchiness.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water, 2 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root, and 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Remove from heat, add 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers, and let steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea, then add honey to taste while still warm.
Drink 2-3 times daily for itchy skin relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the marshmallow plant are rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and relieve itchy skin.
Flowers: The flowers of the marshmallow plant contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation and alleviate itching.
Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant have demulcent properties, which can help to soothe and heal irritated skin.
Seeds: The seeds of the marshmallow plant are rich in mucilage, making them effective in reducing itching and inflammation.
Bark: The bark of the marshmallow plant contains high levels of mucilage, making it an effective remedy for itchy and irritated skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tea helps with itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can soothe and calm irritated skin. Burdock root contains essential fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals that can help to hydrate and nourish the skin, reducing dryness and itchiness. The tea is also known to have natural detoxifying properties that can help to eliminate toxins from the body, which can contribute to skin irritation. Drinking burdock tea or using it as a compress can provide relief from itchy skin, while also promoting overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tea helps reduce itchy skin by decreasing inflammation, which is important because itchiness is often caused by inflammation in the skin; active constituents include tannins and essential fatty acids.
Antipruritic: Burdock tea alleviates itching directly, which is crucial for providing relief to itchy skin; active constituents include quercetin and luteolin.
Antibacterial: Burdock tea inhibits bacterial growth, which is significant because skin infections can exacerbate itchiness; active constituents include arctiin and polyacetylenes.
Demulcent: Burdock tea creates a protective film on the skin, which is beneficial for soothing irritated skin and reducing itchiness; active constituents include mucilage and polysaccharides.

Diuretic: Burdock tea promotes the elimination of toxins from the body, which is important for overall skin health and can help reduce itchiness caused by toxin buildup; active constituents include quercetin and tannins.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 quart of water and 2 tablespoons of dried burdock root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the burdock root, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 2-3 cups of burdock tea daily to soothe itchy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are commonly used in medicinal teas for itchy skin due to their anti-inflammatory and blood purifying properties.
Leaves: Burdock leaves can be used in teas for itchy skin, as they have been known to possess soothing and demulcent qualities, which may help alleviate irritation.
Flowers: The flowers of the burdock plant are used in teas for itchy skin because of their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce skin inflammation.
Seeds: Burdock seeds are often included in medicinal teas for itchy skin, as they contain beneficial oils and fatty acids that can help moisturize and soothe irritated skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with itchy skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and heal irritated skin. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which is rich in flavonoids and carotenoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote skin repair. Drinking pot marigold tea can help to reduce inflammation from the inside out, while applying the tea topically as a compress can provide immediate relief to itchy skin. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation, while its antimicrobial properties can help to prevent infection and promote healing. Overall, pot marigold tea is a natural and effective remedy for itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce itchiness by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antipruritic: The tea has a soothing effect on itchy skin due to its antipruritic properties, which help alleviate the sensation of itching.
Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tea contains antimicrobial compounds, such as lutein and quercetin, that help maintain skin health and prevent skin infections that may cause itching.
Wound healing: The tea promotes wound healing, reducing the risk of skin irritation and itching caused by open wounds or sores, due to its active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils.
Skin hydration: Pot marigold tea helps maintain skin hydration, preventing dryness and itching, by providing antioxidants and essential oils that support the skin's natural barrier function.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried marigold flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the marigold flowers, then let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in honey to taste, if desired.
Drink 1-2 cups of the marigold tea daily to soothe itchy skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in flavonoids and essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them ideal for treating itchy skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold contain antioxidants and are known for their calming effects on the skin, providing relief for itchiness.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids and are often used in skincare for their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchy skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and heal irritated skin. Chamomile contains flavonoids, which are known to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. These properties can help reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief from itchy skin. Additionally, chamomile has been shown to have a calming effect on the skin, which can help alleviate symptoms of skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis that can cause itchy skin. Drinking chamomile tea or applying it topically as a compress can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation, providing much-needed relief for those suffering from itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce skin inflammation and irritation, providing relief for itchy skin. This is due to the presence of active constituents like chamazulene and bisabolol.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help protect the skin from harmful bacteria and fungi, preventing infections that can cause itchiness. The main antimicrobial compounds include alpha-bisabolol, matricin, and azulenes.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tea contains potent antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting skin health and reducing itchiness. The primary antioxidants include apigenin, luteolin, and quercetin.

Wound healing: The wound-healing properties of German chamomile tea can help soothe and heal irritated skin, reducing itchiness and discomfort. Key constituents responsible for this property include the flavonoids apigenin and luteolin, as well as the volatile oils azulene and bisabolol.

Skin barrier repair: German chamomile tea can help repair the skin's natural barrier, which can become compromised in cases of itchy skin. This is achieved through the synergistic effects of various active constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried German chamomile flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the spent chamomile flowers, leaving behind only the liquid.
Let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature, ensuring it's not too hot for skin application.
Apply the cooled tea directly to the itchy skin using a clean cloth or by soaking the affected area in the tea for 10-15 minutes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for soothing itchy skin.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids that have a calming effect on the skin, providing relief from itchiness.
Seeds: The seeds are a great source of essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and skin-soothing properties, making them useful for treating itchy skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with itchy skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and heal irritated skin. Horsetail is rich in silica, a mineral that can strengthen connective tissues and improve the overall health of the skin. Additionally, horsetail tea has been shown to have a cooling effect on the skin, which can provide relief from itching and discomfort. The tea can be applied topically to the affected area or consumed as a drink to promote overall skin health. Horsetail tea is a natural and gentle remedy for itchy skin, making it an attractive alternative to over-the-counter creams and medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness associated with various skin conditions.
Astringent: Horsetail tea has astringent properties due to its tannin content, helping to tighten skin pores, dry out oozing lesions, and relieve itching.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of horsetail tea, attributed to phenolic acids and flavonoids, can help control skin infections that may cause itchiness.
Antioxidant: Horsetail tea is rich in antioxidants, such as silicic acid and flavonoids, which help protect skin cells from oxidative stress, promoting skin health and reducing itchiness.

Nutrient-rich: Horsetail tea contains essential minerals, like silica and selenium, which support skin health by promoting collagen production and maintaining skin elasticity, thereby alleviating itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for every 2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb.
Add the horsetail herb to the boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the horsetail herb, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 2-3 cups of the horsetail tea daily to soothe itchy skin.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes are rich in silica and antioxidants, making them useful for soothing itchy skin and promoting skin health.
Stems: Horsetail stems contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce itching and irritation on the skin.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves are a natural source of minerals and antioxidants, which can help alleviate itchy skin and support skin repair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief to itchy skin. It contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage and promote healing. Fennel tea also has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent skin infections, which can cause itchiness. Furthermore, it has a cooling effect that can soothe the skin and alleviate itchiness. Drinking fennel tea can also help detoxify the body, eliminating toxins that can cause skin issues and itchiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea can reduce inflammation, which is important for itchy skin as it can alleviate the discomfort and redness caused by scratching. This property is attributed to the presence of volatile oils such as anethole, fenchone, and limonene.

Antipruritic: Fennel tea has the ability to relieve itching, which is crucial for itchy skin as it can help break the itch-scratch cycle. The rosmarinic acid and flavonoids in fennel are responsible for this antipruritic effect.

Antioxidant: Fennel tea has high antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for itchy skin as antioxidants can support skin health and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants in fennel tea include rosmarinic acid, quercetin, and kaempferol.

Antimicrobial: Fennel tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent skin infections that can worsen itchy skin. This property is important for itchy skin as it can help maintain skin health and reduce the risk of complications. The antimicrobial properties of fennel tea are attributed to its volatile oils, including trans-anethole and fenchone.

Moisturizing: Fennel tea has moisturizing properties that can help soothe dry and itchy skin. This is important for itchy skin as it can help hydrate and nourish the skin, reducing the need for scratching. The moisturizing properties of fennel tea are attributed to its content of polysaccharides, which can form a protective layer on the skin and retain moisture.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of crushed fennel seeds to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the fennel seeds.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for itchy skin relief.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe itchy skin.
Leaves: Fennel leaves have antipruritic properties that can alleviate itching and provide relief for irritated skin.
Flowers: Fennel flowers contain flavonoids with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce skin irritation and itchiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with itchy skin because it contains an active compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the skin, providing relief from itchiness. Additionally, licorice tea has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are inflammatory compounds that can contribute to itchy skin. Furthermore, the tea has demulcent properties, meaning it can form a protective film over the skin, helping to soothe and hydrate itchy, dry skin. Drinking licorice tea or applying it topically as a compress may provide relief for individuals experiencing itchy skin due to conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, or insect bites.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea helps reduce itchy skin by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents, glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.
Antipruritic: Licorice tea alleviates itchy skin by inhibiting the release of pruritogenic factors, due to the presence of glycyrrhetinic acid.
Immunomodulatory: Licorice tea regulates the immune response, reducing skin irritation and itching, because of the active compound, glycyrrhizin.
Antioxidant: Licorice tea protects the skin from oxidative stress and damage, promoting skin health and reducing itchiness, due to its high flavonoid and phenolic content.
Demulcent: Licorice tea forms a soothing film on the skin, providing relief from itching and irritation, owing to its mucilage content.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and strain the tea into a cup, discarding the licorice root solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of the herbal licorice tea daily to soothe itchy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing itchiness and irritation on the skin.

Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to have anti-pruritic (anti-itch) effects, making them a valuable addition to medicinal teas for itchy skin.
Seeds: Licorice seeds contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been found to have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, which can help alleviate itchy skin.

Flowers: The flowers of the licorice plant contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the skin from itchiness and irritation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy tea helps with itchy skin because it contains natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can soothe and relieve irritation. The plant's active compounds, including saponins and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it an effective natural remedy for itchy skin. Additionally, English ivy has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection and promote healing of damaged skin. Drinking English ivy tea or applying it topically as a compress can provide relief from itchiness and discomfort caused by various skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and insect bites.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English ivy tea helps reduce itchiness by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the presence of saponins and flavonoids.
Antipruritic: It contains antipruritic compounds that help alleviate itching directly, making it an ideal remedy for itchy skin.
Antimicrobial: English ivy tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, preventing skin infections that can cause itchiness, due to its antimicrobial properties from saponins and essential oils.
Anti-allergic: It has anti-allergic properties that help control histamine release, which can help relieve itchiness caused by allergic reactions, from flavonoids and other polyphenols.
Moisturizing: English ivy tea contains compounds that help moisturize and soothe dry, itchy skin, providing much-needed relief, thanks to the presence of mucilage and other emollient compounds.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 fresh English ivy leaves and rinse them thoroughly under running water to remove dirt and bacteria.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and place them in a pot, then add 2 cups of water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and let the tea simmer for 10-15 minutes to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves, and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to alleviate itchy skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of English ivy contain saponins, which can help soothe itchy skin.
Stems: Ivy stems also contain saponins and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating itchy skin.
Rhizomes: Ivy rhizomes have been used in traditional medicine to treat skin conditions, including itchy skin, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tea helps with itchy skin because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation. The herb has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help to combat any potential infections that may be contributing to the itchiness. Additionally, St. John's wort tea contains tannins, which can help to dry out weeping or oozing skin, providing relief from itchiness. The tea can be applied topically to the affected area or consumed orally to provide systemic relief. However, it's important to note that St. John's wort can interact with certain medications, so it's always best to consult with a healthcare provider before using it as a treatment for itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tea contains hypericin and hyperforin, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchy skin.
Antimicrobial: The presence of hyperforin in St. John's wort tea helps fight bacteria and maintain skin health, preventing skin infections that can cause itching.
Antioxidant: St. John's wort tea is rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect skin cells from damage, reducing itchiness.
Analgesic: The tea's active constituents, including hypericin, can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with itchy skin.
Wound healing: St. John's wort tea promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can help soothe itchy skin and support overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried St. John's Wort flowers and 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the herb's properties.
Strain the tea to remove the spent flowers and retain the liquid.
Discard the used herb and drink the tea while it is still warm.
Repeat the process up to three times a day for itchy skin relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective for soothing itchy skin.
Leaves: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve itchiness.
Buds: Contain high concentrations of active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects on the skin.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy skin with herbal teas is by using them as a compress. Chamomile and calendula teas are particularly effective, as they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation and itching. To prepare, steep 1-2 tea bags in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then let it cool. Once cooled, soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply it to the affected area for 10-15 minutes, several times a day. This can help soothe and relieve itchiness, while also promoting skin healing.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy skin that are treated with herbal teas include eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Chamomile tea is often used for its anti-inflammatory properties to soothe irritated skin, while nettle tea can help alleviate allergic reactions that cause itching. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, can reduce inflammation and improve skin health. Licorice root tea is known to have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, making it beneficial for various skin conditions. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any herbal remedy.
